rulings.Case Title: TRUJILLO, MARY ANN, AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO et al VS. DURO DYNE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 7, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Speck was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories were not factually devoid and, specifically, they identified Robert Cantley as a person with knowledge and identified what knowledge he has and defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Cantley does not have such knowledge. Defendant also failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs stipulated that Mr. Cantley would not be offered as a witness against it. Additionally, were the court to get to plaintiffs' papers, Mr. Cantley's deposition testimony submitted by plaintiffs would be further grounds to conclude that defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is off calendar as moot as plaintiffs have dismissed such claim. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
